twitter_thread: Groq just raised $650M, but 80% of AI inference workloads can run on $500 NVIDIA GPUs.
Optimizing AI models with TensorFlow Lite can cut latency by 30% and costs by 25%.
Use tools like TensorFlow Lite and OpenVINO to optimize your models before scaling.
Switching to spot instances can cut cloud costs by 50% for non-critical AI workloads.
On-prem hardware like AMD's EPYC can be 30% cheaper than cloud for large-scale AI deployments.
Avoid over-provisioning resources: a single unnecessary GPU can add $10k/yr to your bill.
What's the most creative way you've optimized your AI workload for cost? #ai #inference
pack.linkedin: Groq's $650M raise highlights the demand for AI inference, but most models can run on $500 NVIDIA GPUs with proper optimization using TensorFlow Lite and OpenVINO. Switching to spot instances can cut cloud costs by 50% and on-prem hardware like AMD's EPYC can be 30% cheaper than cloud. What strategies have you used to optimize AI workloads and reduce costs by 25% or more?
tiktok_hooks: Groq raised 650 million, but you can run AI models on a 500-dollar GPU

pack.reddit_headline: Groq's $650M raise: justification for pricey AI hardware or unnecessary hype?
